sections.paragraph:
σεαυτοῦ (not σαυτοῦ [so Jos., Ant. 18, 336; Tat. 25, 1], B-D-F §31, 1 and 64, 1; Mlt-H. 180f; Rob. 287), ῆς (Alcaeus [c. 600 b.c.] et al.; pap, LXX, En, JosAs; ParJer 6:6; EpArist, Just., Tat.) reflexive pron. of the second pers. sing. (Kühner-Bl. I 596ff; B-D-F §283; W-S. §22, 11; Rob. 687–90) used only in the gen., dat., and acc. (on the replacement of σεαυτοῦ by ἑαυτοῦ s. ἑαυτοῦ 1b) yourself

ⓐ gen. Mt 18:16 v.l.; J 1:22; 8:13; 9:17 א* P75 (EBammel, NTS 40, ’94, 455f); 2 Ti 4:11; Hv 3, 6, 7; m 1:2.

ⓑ dat. J 17:5; Ac 9:34; 16:28; Ro 2:5; Hm 3:5; 9:1b; 12, 3, 5f. Also λογίζεσθαι ἐν σεαυτῷ Hs 9, 2, 6 (a Semitism, cp. TestJob 2:3; Leutzsch, Hermas 486 n. 290).

ⓒ acc. Mt 4:6; 8:4; Mk 1:44; Lk 5:14; Ro 2:21; Gal 6:1; 1 Ti 4:16b; B 19:3; Dg 2:1; Hm 9:8.—On the quot. fr. Lev 19:18: Mt 19:19; 22:39; Mk 12:31; Lk 10:27; D 1:2, cp. Appian, Bell. Civ. 3, 75 §305: Pansa says to Octavian ἐγὼ τῷ σῷ πατρὶ φίλος ἦν ὡς ἐμαυτῷ; Vi. Aesopi W 31 P.: θέλω (q.v. 3b=love) αὐτὴν (his wife) ὡς ἐμαυτόν=ed. Eberh. I c. 8 p. 247, 1 ταύτην ὡς ἐμαυτὸν στέργω.—M-M.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Alcaeus Alcaeus , lyric poet, VII–VI b.c.—List 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

pron. pron. = pronoun

pers. pers. = person(s)

sing. sing. = singular

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

gen. gen. = genitive

dat. dat. = dative

acc. acc. = accusative

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Leutzsch, Hermas Leutzsch, Hermas = MLeutzsch, Hirt des Hermas—Lists 1 (s. H), 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

quot. quot. = quotation

  1. fr. = from

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Vi. Aesopi Vi. Aesopi , = Vitae Aesopi, I a.d.—List 5

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 917
